deep_dive.title: $\text{Log}_\text{b}$Quant: Quantizing Language Models in Logarithmic Space
deep_dive.abstract: Quantization has become an invaluable tool to reduce memory requirements and inference speed of modern language models, in particular to make them available for consumer setups and edge devices. While previous work has primarily focused on uniform quantization codebooks, such approaches are prone to suboptimal representations due to low-frequency high-magnitude weights. We introduce Log$_\text{b}$Quant, a novel logarithmic quantization approach with adjustable bases, to adapt to common parameter distributions. We show that our method exhibits superior performance at 4-bit precision on several performance benchmarks compared to asymmetric linear quantization at tensor-wise granularity, while achieving moderate speedup and high memory savings, making it suitable for private use on consumer-grade GPUs.
deep_dive.llm_evidence_en: proposes logarithmic quantization for language models improving inference efficiency
deep_dive.llm_evidence_cn: 提出对数域量化方法提升语言模型推理效率
deep_dive.llm_evidence: 提出对数域量化方法提升语言模型推理效率
deep_dive.canonical_evidence: 提出对数域量化方法提升语言模型推理效率
deep_dive.llm_tldr_en: Log_bQuant introduces a logarithmic quantization approach with adjustable bases to adapt to parameter distributions, achieving superior 4-bit performance and memory savings on language models, directly addressing weight quantization for LLMs.
deep_dive.llm_tldr_cn: 该论文提出Log_bQuant，一种基于对数域的可调基量化方法，解决了均匀量化在高幅值低频权重上表示不佳的问题。通过在语言模型上以4比特精度进行张量级量化，该方法在多个基准测试中优于非对称线性量化，同时实现了中等加速和高内存节省，直接服务于大型语言模型的权重量化需求，为高效推理提供支持。
deep_dive.llm_tldr: 该论文提出Log_bQuant，一种基于对数域的可调基量化方法，解决了均匀量化在高幅值低频权重上表示不佳的问题。通过在语言模型上以4比特精度进行张量级量化，该方法在多个基准测试中优于非对称线性量化，同时实现了中等加速和高内存节省，直接服务于大型语言模型的权重量化需求，为高效推理提供支持。
deep_dive.matched_query_text: weight quantization for large language models

quick_skim.title: Learning to Select, Not Relearn: Hard-Routed Mixtures of Reasoning LoRAs
quick_skim.abstract: Composing independently trained LoRA adapters into a single large language model is useful for multi-domain adaptation, especially when the original training data cannot be shared. A common approach is to use MoE-style routing over LoRA experts, but for frozen pretrained adapters, soft weighted combinations can change the unit-scale additive update under which each LoRA module was originally trained. We propose \textbf{Hard-Routed MoR-LoRA}, a two-stage framework for composing frozen reasoning LoRA experts through unit-scale hard selection. First, domain-specific LoRA adapters are trained independently using reinforcement learning from verifiable feedback to obtain reasoning experts. Then, all experts are frozen, reasoning traces are distilled from them, and only a lightweight shared router together with a small attention LoRA is trained for integration. The router selects exactly one expert per token using hard top-1 routing, while a straight-through estimator enables gradient-based training. Experiments across five benchmarks, multiple model scales, and additional model families show that Hard-Routed MoR-LoRA preserves expert behavior while requiring substantially fewer trainable parameters than soft-routing mixture baselines. Our analysis further shows that normalized soft mixtures often concentrate most routing mass on a single expert, suggesting that hard unit-scale routing provides a simple and efficient abstraction for frozen LoRA expert composition.
quick_skim.llm_evidence_en: hard-routed MoE inference for LoRA experts
quick_skim.llm_tldr_en: Proposes Hard-Routed MoR-LoRA that composes frozen reasoning LoRA experts via unit-scale hard selection, enabling efficient MoE-style inference without soft weight degradation.
quick_skim.llm_tldr_cn: 组合独立训练的LoRA适配器时，软加权组合会破坏各适配器的原始单位尺度。本文提出硬路由MoR-LoRA框架，采用两阶段训练：先独立训练领域特定LoRA推理专家，再冻结所有专家并通过蒸馏推理轨迹进行硬选择。该方法避免了软组合的尺度变化，实现了高效的MoE式推理。实验证明硬路由在保持专家能力的同时提升了推理效率。
quick_skim.llm_tldr: 组合独立训练的LoRA适配器时，软加权组合会破坏各适配器的原始单位尺度。本文提出硬路由MoR-LoRA框架，采用两阶段训练：先独立训练领域特定LoRA推理专家，再冻结所有专家并通过蒸馏推理轨迹进行硬选择。该方法避免了软组合的尺度变化，实现了高效的MoE式推理。实验证明硬路由在保持专家能力的同时提升了推理效率。
quick_skim.matched_query_text: efficient mixture of experts inference techniques

quick_skim.title: Breaking the Rounding Trap: Securing LLMs against Quantization-Conditioned Backdoors
quick_skim.abstract: Model quantization is a key technique for reducing storage and inference costs when deploying large language models in practice. However, recent studies show that the discretization and rounding errors introduced by quantization can be exploited by adversaries to construct quantization-conditioned backdoor (QCB) attacks. Under such attacks, malicious behaviors remain dormant in the full-precision stage and are activated only after quantized deployment, thereby bypassing conventional security auditing and detection mechanisms. To address this threat, we propose a proactive pre-quantization defense method, QuantGuard. Our method introduces differentiable rounding control variables and combines error-guided rounding reversal constraints, output-distribution consistency, and weight-distance regularization to finely regulate critical rounding behaviors. Crucially, QuantGuard utilizes only a small calibration dataset and does not modify existing quantization algorithms. This design breaks the precise alignment between attacker-crafted weight patterns and quantization boundaries, effectively suppressing the post-quantization backdoor activation pathway while preserving the model's original functionality and performance. We conduct systematic experiments on six mainstream LLMs (including the LLaMA-3 and Qwen2.5-Coder) using three quantization precisions (INT8, FP4, and NF4) across three representative scenarios: vulnerable code generation, content injection, and over-refusal. The results show that QuantGuard consistently mitigates QCB attacks, reducing the attack success rate to a level comparable to the clean model while largely preserving performance on general capability benchmarks. With low computational overhead, our method offers an effective, practical solution for secure quantized LLM deployment.
quick_skim.llm_evidence_en: quantization for LLMs, addresses rounding errors
quick_skim.llm_evidence_cn: 针对大语言模型的量化，处理舍入误差
quick_skim.llm_evidence: 针对大语言模型的量化，处理舍入误差
quick_skim.canonical_evidence: 针对大语言模型的量化，处理舍入误差
quick_skim.llm_tldr_en: Proposes QuantGuard, a proactive pre-quantization defense method using differentiable rounding control to secure LLMs against quantization-conditioned backdoors.
quick_skim.llm_tldr_cn: 模型量化部署存在被后门攻击的风险，量化舍入误差可被利用。本文提出预处理防御方法QuantGuard，引入可微舍入控制变量并结合误差引导舍入反转约束，在量化前阻断后门激活。该方法在不影响模型精度前提下提升量化模型安全性。
quick_skim.llm_tldr: 模型量化部署存在被后门攻击的风险，量化舍入误差可被利用。本文提出预处理防御方法QuantGuard，引入可微舍入控制变量并结合误差引导舍入反转约束，在量化前阻断后门激活。该方法在不影响模型精度前提下提升量化模型安全性。
quick_skim.matched_query_text: weight quantization for large language models
